revision.text:
北山茶臼山古墳は群馬県富岡市にある古墳。 ここでは、本古墳と関わりが深い北山茶臼山西古墳についても書く。
{{日本の古墳| 名称=北山茶臼山古墳| 所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99| 形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり)| 規模=直径約40m| 年代=[[4世紀]]前半| }}
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、[[富岡市]]の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[明治27年]]に発掘された。出土品の多くは紛失し、現在本古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== 不明(竪穴系)
==出土品== 不明 [[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳| 名称=北山茶臼山西古墳| 所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]下高瀬| 形状=前方後方墳| 規模=28m| 年代=[[4世紀]]前半| }}
現在は破壊されたため、詳細不明。[[昭和61年]]に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]で、[[銅鏡]]2面、鉄矛などが出土した。現在出土品は[[富岡市]]立額部小学校に保管されている。

revision.text:
北山茶臼山古墳は群馬県富岡市にある古墳。 ここでは、本古墳と関わりが深い北山茶臼山西古墳についても書く。
{{日本の古墳| 名称=北山茶臼山古墳| 所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99| 形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり)| 規模=直径約40m| 年代=[[4世紀]]前半| }}
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、[[富岡市]]の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[明治27年]]に発掘された。出土品の多くは紛失し、現在本古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== 不明(竪穴系)
==出土品== 不明 [[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳| 名称=北山茶臼山西古墳| 所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]下高瀬| 形状=前方後方墳| 規模=28m| 年代=[[4世紀]]前半| }}
現在は破壊されたため、詳細不明。[[昭和61年]]に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]で、[[銅鏡]]2面、鉄矛などが出土した。現在出土品は[[富岡市]]立額部小学校に保管されている。 {{デフォルトソート:きたやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 体裁を調整。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99 |形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり) |規模=直径約40m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} '''北山茶臼山古墳'''（きたやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]にある[[古墳]]。本記事では、同古墳と関わりが深い'''[[#北山茶臼山西古墳|北山茶臼山西古墳]]'''（きたやまちゃうすやまにしこふん）についても述べる。
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、富岡市の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）に発掘されたが出土品の多くは紛失し、現在同古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== *不明（竪穴系）
==出土品== 三角縁神獣鏡1面（宮内庁所蔵）を除き、多くは不明である。
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山西古墳 |所在地=群馬県富岡市下高瀬 |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=28m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} 北山茶臼山西古墳は破壊されたため、詳細不明である。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]で、[[銅鏡]]2面、鉄矛などが出土した。現在出土品は富岡市立額部小学校に保管されている。
{{デフォルトソート:きたやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99 |形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり) |規模=直径約40m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} '''北山茶臼山古墳'''（きたやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]にある[[古墳]]。本記事では、同古墳と関わりが深い'''[[#北山茶臼山西古墳|北山茶臼山西古墳]]'''（きたやまちゃうすやまにしこふん）についても述べる。
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、富岡市の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）に発掘されたが出土品の多くは紛失し、現在同古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== *不明（竪穴系）
==出土品== 三角縁神獣鏡1面（宮内庁所蔵）を除き、多くは不明である。
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山西古墳 |所在地=群馬県富岡市下高瀬 |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=28m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半～半ば |}} 北山茶臼山西古墳は破壊されたため、詳細不明である。 全長28m、後方部一辺18m、前方部幅17m。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]直葬。昭和30年代に開墾のため墳丘が大きく崩され、その時、変形四獣鏡が出土した。消滅前の発掘では、埋葬施設周辺から[[銅鏡]]、鉄矛、[[ガラス]]小玉、農具が出土した。現在出土品は富岡市立額部小学校に保管されている。
甘楽富岡地域では最も早く出現した古墳とされることが多いが、実際は富岡市宇田の安曽岡遺跡2号墳(4世紀初頭～前半)である。
{{デフォルトソート:きたやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99 |形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり) |規模=直径約40m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} '''北山茶臼山古墳'''（きたやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]にある[[古墳]]。本記事では、同古墳と関わりが深い'''[[#北山茶臼山西古墳|北山茶臼山西古墳]]'''（きたやまちゃうすやまにしこふん）についても述べる。
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、富岡市の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）に発掘されたが出土品の多くは紛失し、現在同古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== *不明（竪穴系）
==出土品== 三角縁神獣鏡1面（宮内庁所蔵）を除き、多くは不明である。
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山西古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=群馬県富岡市下高瀬 |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=28m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半～半ば |}} 北山茶臼山西古墳は破壊されたため、詳細不明である。 全長28m、後方部一辺18m、前方部幅17m。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]直葬。昭和30年代に開墾のため墳丘が大きく崩され、その時、変形四獣鏡が出土した。消滅前の発掘では、埋葬施設周辺から[[銅鏡]]、鉄矛、[[ガラス]]小玉、農具が出土した。現在出土品は富岡市立額部小学校に保管されている。
甘楽富岡地域では最も早く出現した古墳とされることが多いが、実際は富岡市宇田の安曽岡遺跡2号墳(4世紀初頭～前半)である。
{{デフォルトソート:きたやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Created page with "== Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly exca..."
revision.text: == Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}}|| Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|ja|会津若松市}} || || 4th century || Among the oldest in Tohoku, designated as a National Historic Site in 1972. |- ! colspan=5 | Kanto Region |- | {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Burial Mound|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Two Four-God Four-Beast Mirrors excavated. |- | {{ill|Tamonoko Burial Mound|ja|頼母子古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}} || Unknown (Keyhole-shaped mound is likely) || Mid-4th century || Two triangular-rimmed mirrors (one location unknown). |- | {{ill|Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound|ja|北山茶臼山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Tomioka|ja|富岡市}} || Round mound (possible keyhole shape) || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe == Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound|ja|北山茶臼山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Tomioka|ja|富岡市}} || Round mound (possible keyhole shape) || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}} || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|ja|真土大塚山古墳}} (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|ja|平塚市}}, Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|ja|白山古墳}} (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, Saiwai Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- ! colspan=5 | Chubu Region |- | {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Burial Mound|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} || Yamanashi Prefecture, {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}, Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Daimaruyama Burial {{ill|Fukui|ja|福井市}} || Round mound || || Discovered in September 2000. |- | {{ill|Otanaka Crown Prince Burial Mound|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} || Ishikawa Prefecture, {{ill|Noto District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}} || Moon" inscription. |- | {{ill|Akamongami Burial Mound|ja|赤門上古墳}} || Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early 4th century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound, Mound|ja|連福寺山古墳}} || Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Three-God Five-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Kamihirakawa Ōtsuka Burial Mound|ja|上平川大塚古墳}} || Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kikugawa, Shizuoka|ja|菊川市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || One mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon," along with two other mirrors |- | {{ill|Gōōdōyama Burial revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] to [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] to [[Category:kofun]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Kofun]] to [[Category:Individual Kofun]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Individual Kofun]] to [[Category:kkkk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Individual Kofun]] to [[Category:kkkkk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !!
