{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Individual Kofun]] to [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in **Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan**. This article also describes the closely related **[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]** (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a **two-tier round burial mound (kofun)** situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was **excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)**; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is **a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)**, which is currently housed in the **[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection**.
== Burial Chamber == * **Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)**
== Excavated Artifacts == * **One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror** (Currently held by the **Imperial Household Agency**) * Other artifacts are **unknown due to loss**.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **destroyed**, and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was **28m in length**, with a **rear section width of 18m** and a **front section width of 17m**. The kofun was excavated in **1986 (Showa 61)**.
Its burial facility consisted of a **direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)**. During the **1950s (Showa 30s)**, the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which **a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)** was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * **Bronze mirrors** * **Iron halberd (鉄矛)** * **Glass beads** * **Agricultural tools**
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at **Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School**.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was **previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region**, but more recent studies indicate that **Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)** in **Uda, Tomika City**, dating to the **early 4th century**, may be older.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tomika City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.

Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.

Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.

Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).

It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.

Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.

Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---

Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|Image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = Round Kofun (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | size = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | size = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] burial mound located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round burial mound (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated artifacts were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== Burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== Excavated Artifacts == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other artifacts are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---

Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a deformed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated artifacts are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
