{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image募集中|cat=富岡市|12=draft}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image募集中|cat=富岡市|12=draft}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
{{citation needed|Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun}}
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99 |形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり) |規模=直径約40m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} '''北山茶臼山古墳'''（きたやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]にある[[古墳]]。本記事では、同古墳と関わりが深い'''[[#北山茶臼山西古墳|北山茶臼山西古墳]]'''（きたやまちゃうすやまにしこふん）についても述べる。
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、富岡市の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）に発掘されたが出土品の多くは紛失し、現在同古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== *不明（竪穴系）
==出土品== 三角縁神獣鏡1面（宮内庁所蔵）を除き、多くは不明である。
==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山西古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=群馬県富岡市下高瀬 |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=28m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半～半ば |}} 北山茶臼山西古墳は破壊されたため、詳細不明である。 全長28m、後方部一辺18m、前方部幅17m。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]直葬。昭和30年代に開墾のため墳丘が大きく崩され、その時、変形四獣鏡が出土した。消滅前の発掘では、埋葬施設周辺から[[銅鏡]]、鉄矛、[[ガラス]]小玉、農具が出土した。現在出土品は富岡市立額部小学校に保管されている。
甘楽富岡地域では最も早く出現した古墳とされることが多いが、実際は富岡市宇田の安曽岡遺跡2号墳(4世紀初頭～前半)である。
{{デフォルトソート:きたやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]南後箇99 |形状=円墳(前方後円墳の可能性あり) |規模=直径約40m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |}} '''北山茶臼山古墳'''（きたやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[富岡市]]にある[[古墳]]。本記事では、同古墳と関わりが深い'''[[#北山茶臼山西古墳|北山茶臼山西古墳]]'''（きたやまちゃうすやまにしこふん）についても述べる。
==概要== 北山茶臼山古墳は、富岡市の丘陵上にある二段構成の[[円墳]]で、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）に発掘されたが出土品の多くは紛失し、現在同古墳出土と確定できるのは[[宮内庁]]が所蔵する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]1面のみである。
==埋葬施設== *不明（竪穴系）

==北山茶臼山西古墳== {{日本の古墳 |名称=北山茶臼山西古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=富岡市}} |所在地=群馬県富岡市下高瀬 |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=28m |年代=[[4世紀]]前半～半ば |}} 北山茶臼山西古墳は破壊されたため、詳細不明である。 全長28m、後方部一辺18m、前方部幅17m。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）に発掘された。埋葬施設は[[木棺]]直葬。昭和30年代に開墾のため墳丘が大きく崩され、その時、変形四獣鏡が出土した。消滅前の発掘では、埋葬施設周辺から[[銅鏡]]、鉄矛、[[ガラス]]小玉、農具が出土した。現在出土品は富岡市立額部小学校に保管されている。
甘楽富岡地域では最も早く出現した古墳とされることが多いが、実際は富岡市宇田の安曽岡遺跡2号墳(4世紀初頭～前半)である。

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]
{{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.

== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.

Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.

== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''

== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.

== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]
