'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tomioka|WD=Q873835}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877306]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tomioka|WD=Q873835}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北山茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tomioka|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = [[Tomika, Gunma|Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture]] | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Possible Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Diameter approx. 40m | period = Early 4th century }}
'''Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (北山茶臼山古墳) is an [[kofun]] Kofun located in '''Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan'''. This article also describes the closely related '''[[#Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun|Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun]]''' (北山茶臼山西古墳).
== Overview == Kitayama Chausuyama Kofun is a '''two-tier round Kofun (kofun)''' situated on a hill in Tomika City. It was '''excavated in 1894 (Meiji 27)'''; however, most of its excavated burial goods were lost over time. The only remaining confirmed artifact from this kofun is '''a single triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror (三角縁神獣鏡)''', which is currently housed in the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} collection'''.
== burial Chamber == * '''Unclear (Possibly a pit-style chamber)'''
== burial goods == * '''One triangular-rimmed deity and beast mirror''' (Currently held by the '''Imperial Household Agency''') * Other burial goods are '''unknown due to loss'''.
---
== Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun == {{Infobox kofun | name = Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun | image = {{ill|image requested|ja|image requested|cat=富岡市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image requested|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = Tomika City, Gunma Prefecture | type = Rectangular Keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後方墳) | area = 28m | period = Early to mid-4th century }}
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''destroyed''', and therefore, its details remain unclear. It was '''28m in length''', with a '''rear section width of 18m''' and a '''front section width of 17m'''. The kofun was excavated in '''1986 (Showa 61)'''.
Its burial facility consisted of a '''direct wooden coffin burial (木棺直葬)'''. During the '''1950s (Showa 30s)''', the mound was heavily damaged due to farmland development, during which '''a detypeed four-beast mirror (変形四獣鏡)''' was unearthed.
Before its complete destruction, the excavation recovered: * '''Bronze mirrors''' * '''Iron halberd (鉄矛)''' * '''Glass beads''' * '''Agricultural tools'''
Currently, the excavated burial goods are preserved at '''Tomika City Nagakabe Elementary School'''.
Kitayama Chausuyama Nishi Kofun was '''previously considered the oldest kofun in the Kanra-Tomika region''', but more recent studies indicate that '''Asooka Site No. 2 Kofun (安曽岡遺跡2号墳)''' in '''Uda, Tomika City''', dating to the '''early 4th century''', may be older.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]
